# Route Stop

> **Status:** DRAFT — pending review before release

## Definition

A single scheduled visit instance generated from a route assignment. A route stop represents "tech X is expected at location Y on date Z." It is the planned event; the service stop is the actual work performed. Understanding the distinction between route stop (planned) and service stop (completed) is critical for calculating completion rates, skip rates, and scheduling accuracy.

## System of Record

**Primary:** Skimmer
**Rationale:** Route stops are auto-generated by Skimmer from route assignments. They are managed through the Skimmer web and mobile apps. No other system tracks visit-level scheduling.

## Synonyms

| Term | System | Context |
|------|--------|---------|
| RouteStop | Skimmer | Database entity — the planned visit |
| Stop | Skimmer UI | Shorthand used by techs ("my next stop") |
| Visit | Business | Informal term for a scheduled stop |
| stg_route_stop | Data Warehouse | Staging model |
| fact_service_stop | Data Warehouse | Warehouse fact — combines RouteStop + ServiceStop data |
| — | QuickBooks Online | No equivalent |
| — | Zoho CRM | No equivalent |

## Naming Convention

**Standard format:** Route stops are identified by date + location + technician.

**Rules:**
- Route stops inherit the `Sequence` from their parent route assignment, determining visit order
- A `RouteMove` changes the scheduled date or assigned tech for a single stop without modifying the recurring assignment

## Identifiers

| System | Field | Format | Example |
|--------|-------|--------|---------|
| Skimmer | RouteStop.id | UUID | `a3b4c5d6e7f8...` |
| Data Warehouse | route_stop_id + _company_name | UUID + text | `a3b4c5d6...` + `AQPS` |

## Key Business Rules

- **Generated from RouteAssignment:** Skimmer creates route stops automatically based on the assignment's day-of-week and frequency. Techs do not manually create route stops.
- **One RouteStop → many ServiceStops:** A location with 3 pools generates 1 RouteStop and 3 ServiceStops (one per pool).
- **Completion tracking:**
  - `StartTime` = tech tapped "Start" in the mobile app
  - `CompleteTime` = tech tapped "Complete"
  - Sentinel: `CompleteTime = '2010-01-01 12:00:00'` = NOT completed
- **Skip tracking:**
  - `IsSkipped = 1` marks a stop as skipped
  - `SkippedStopReasonId` links to a reason (weather, gate locked, customer request, holiday, etc.)
  - Company settings can require a reason when skipping
- **Biweekly off-week:** `IsOffBiweekly` flag indicates an off-week for biweekly schedules. These stops exist in the system but are not expected to be completed.
- **Route moves:** A `RouteMove` changes the date or tech for a single stop. The moved stop gets a `RouteMoveId` linking back to the move record.
- **Customer notifications:**
  - `EmailHeader` and `EmailMessage` define the service report email
  - `EmailStatus` tracks delivery (Sent, Failed, etc.)
  - `EmailSentDate` records when the notification was sent
- **Service photos:** `PhotoUrl` links to photos taken by the tech as documentation

## Data Flow

```
Skimmer (RouteAssignment → generates RouteStop per frequency)
    │
    ├── Normal completion:
    │   └── Tech starts/completes → ServiceStop records per pool
    │
    ├── Skipped:
    │   └── IsSkipped = 1, SkippedStopReasonId set
    │
    ├── Moved:
    │   └── RouteMove created → new RouteStop on different date/tech
    │
    └── Nightly SQLite Extract
        └── Data Warehouse
            ├── public_staging.stg_route_stop (raw: all route stop fields)
            └── public_warehouse.fact_service_stop (joined with service stop data)
```

## Field Mapping

| Canonical Field | Skimmer | Warehouse | Notes |
|----------------|---------|-----------|-------|
| Route Stop ID | id | route_stop_id | UUID primary key |
| Assignment | RouteAssignmentId | — | FK to RouteAssignment (not in fact table) |
| Location | ServiceLocationId | service_location_id | FK to Service Location |
| Technician | AccountId | tech_id | FK to Account / dim_tech |
| Service Date | ServiceDate | service_date | TEXT in warehouse |
| Start Time | StartTime | start_time | When tech arrived |
| Complete Time | CompleteTime | complete_time | Sentinel for incomplete |
| Minutes | MinutesAtStop | minutes_at_stop | Duration |
| Skipped | IsSkipped | — | Boolean |
| Skip Reason | SkippedStopReasonId | — | FK to SkippedStopReason |
| Off Biweekly | IsOffBiweekly | — | Biweekly off-week flag |
| Moved From | RouteMoveId | — | FK to RouteMove |
| Sequence | Sequence | — | Visit order within route |
| Photo | PhotoUrl | — | Service documentation |
| Email Status | EmailStatus | — | Notification tracking |
| Company | CompanyId | _company_name | AQPS or JOMO |

## Key Metrics Derived from Route Stops

| Metric | Calculation | Business Value |
|--------|-------------|----------------|
| Completion Rate | Completed stops / Total stops (excl. off-biweekly) | Service reliability |
| Skip Rate | Skipped stops / Total stops | Identifies access issues, scheduling problems |
| Average Minutes per Stop | AVG(minutes_at_stop) WHERE service_status = 1 | Workforce planning, route optimization |
| Stops per Day per Tech | COUNT(completed) GROUP BY tech, date | Utilization and capacity |

## Related Entities

- [Route Assignment](route-assignment-DRAFT.md) — many:1 (each stop is generated from an assignment)
- [Service Stop](service-stop-DRAFT.md) — 1:many (one route stop generates one service stop per pool)
- [Technician](technician-DRAFT.md) — many:1 (each stop is assigned to one tech)
- [Service Location](service-location.md) — many:1 (each stop is at one location)
- [Chemical Reading](chemical-reading-DRAFT.md) — indirect via Service Stop (stop → service stop → readings)
- [Invoice](invoice.md) — via InvoiceItem (route stop charges can appear on invoices)
